name: Active Directory Account Unlock
description: Unlocks a locked Active Directory user account using PowerShell. Checks current lockout status, unlocks the account, verifies the unlock succeeded, and displays lockout-related event info.
author: sentinel-desktop

parameters.name: username
parameters.label: Username (sAMAccountName)
parameters.description: The Active Directory username (sAMAccountName) of the locked account to unlock

parameters.name: domain_controller
parameters.label: Domain Controller
parameters.description: Optional: Target a specific domain controller (FQDN). If blank, uses the nearest DC.

parameters.name: reset_bad_password_count
parameters.label: Reset Bad Password Count
parameters.description: Whether to reset the bad password count after unlocking (recommended: true)

steps.description: Open PowerShell with execution policy bypass for AD cmdlets
steps.name: Open PowerShell with execution policy bypass for A

steps.description: Import the Active Directory PowerShell module
steps.name: Import the Active Directory PowerShell module

params.text: $user = Get-ADUser -Identity '{{username}}' -Properties LockedOut, badPwdCount, LastBadPasswordAttempt, AccountLockoutTime -ErrorAction Stop

steps.description: Look up the AD user with lockout-related properties
steps.name: Look up the AD user with lockout-related propertie

params.text: Write-Host "[INFO] Account: {{username}} | Locked Out: $($user.LockedOut) | Bad PWD Count: $($user.badPwdCount)" -ForegroundColor Cyan

steps.description: Display current lockout status and bad password count
steps.name: Display current lockout status and bad password co

params.text: if ($user.LastBadPasswordAttempt) { Write-Host "[INFO] Last bad password attempt: $($user.LastBadPasswordAttempt)" -ForegroundColor Yellow }

steps.description: Show last bad password attempt timestamp if available
steps.name: Show last bad password attempt timestamp if availa

params.text: if (-not $user.LockedOut) { Write-Host "[WARNING] Account '{{username}}' is NOT currently locked out." -ForegroundColor Yellow; exit }

steps.description: Check if account is already unlocked and warn if so
steps.name: Check if account is already unlocked and warn if s

steps.description: Unlock the Active Directory user account
steps.name: Unlock the Active Directory user account

steps.description: Re-fetch the account to verify the unlock succeeded
steps.name: Re-fetch the account to verify the unlock succeede

params.text: if ($verify.LockedOut) { Write-Host '[ERROR] Account is still locked. Check replication or contact domain admin.' -ForegroundColor Red } else { Write-Host '[SUCCESS] Account {{username}} has been unlocked successfully.' -ForegroundColor Green }

steps.description: Display verification result — success or failure
steps.name: Display verification result — success or failure

params.text: $timestamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'; Write-Host "[AUDIT] Account unlock performed at $timestamp for user: {{username}}" -ForegroundColor Magenta

steps.description: Log an audit trail entry with timestamp
steps.name: Log an audit trail entry with timestamp
